operating_state: Total revenue increased to $2.46 billion for the six months ended June 30, 2022, driven by rental revenue growth from positive rental rate growth and development completions. Real Estate Operations NOI increased driven by higher rental revenues while Strategic Capital NOI rose from higher asset management fees and promote revenues. Quarterly dividend payments increased to $0.79 per common share from $0.63 in the prior year period.
cost_structure: Rental expenses increased while maintaining operational efficiency, with general and administrative expenses rising driven by higher equity-based compensation. Interest payments net of capitalized amounts decreased to $142 million from $180 million in the prior year period. The company capitalized $72 million of internal development costs compared to $60 million in the prior year.
material_events: Prologis entered into a definitive merger agreement with Duke Realty Corporation on June 11, 2022, for an estimated acquisition price of approximately $25.5 billion including assumed debt at an exchange ratio of 0.475 Prologis shares per Duke Realty share. Generated net proceeds of $1.9 billion and realized net gains of $901 million from property contributions to European co-investment ventures and dispositions of non-strategic assets. The company established a new $3.0 billion Global Senior Credit Agreement maturing June 2026.
macro_exposures: The company maintains exposure to foreign currency fluctuations through investments across 19 countries, with natural hedging through borrowing in functional currencies and derivative instruments. Interest rate exposure exists on floating rate debt though weighted average effective interest rate remained low at 1.6%. Supply chain disruption risks continue to impact construction costs despite strong market fundamentals.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued strong performance driven by estimated 56% net effective lease mark-to-market potential and robust customer demand outpacing supply. The development portfolio investment has $2.7 billion remaining to be spent with 49% pre-leasing achieved. The Duke Realty acquisition is expected to close by January 2023 subject to approvals and customary closing conditions.
uncertainty_profile: The Duke Realty merger faces standard transaction risks including shareholder approval requirements and potential termination fees. Unconsolidated co-investment ventures carried $7.7 billion in investments with $11.9 billion in third-party debt at 25.7% weighted average loan-to-value ratio. Foreign currency translation risks persist across international operations though partially mitigated through natural hedging strategies.
